title: ChaCha20-Poly1305 and Algorithm Choice · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Aead Cross Implementation
summary: A mobile product chooses an AEAD from benchmark folklore, mixes XChaCha and IETF nonce formats, and assumes changing algorithms repairs nonce reuse. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the aead cross implementation increment in portable AEAD selection and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Verify authoritative vectors and cross-implementation fixtures for exact nonce, AAD, ciphertext, tag, and rejection behavior.
Represent algorithm suite and parameters in an authenticated allow-listed envelope without negotiation downgrade.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Aead Cross Implementation · Theory Crypto Go Aead Cross Imple…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 4853 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to select and verify standard or XChaCha20-Poly1305 with exact sizes, AAD, vectors, and interoperability; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then swap the role, tenant, recipient, algorithm, or context and prove domain separation. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify authoritative vectors and cross-implementation fixtures for exact nonce, AAD, ciphertext, tag, and rejection behavior.
